notes: Standing E2E queries — re-run every iteration. SKILL.md keys on `id`. All file deliverables go to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\ (NOT the desktop). Each query has only an `instruction` (and optional `expect_files`). Verdict is computed by the SKILL: (1) Run layer = manifest.status == 'ok'; (2) Goal layer = a per-thread subagent reads instruction + final_text (+ context.log if needed) and judges whether the task's goal was achieved (goal_met = pass / fail / partial); (3) Deliverables layer = each `expect_files` entry passes its disk check (existence + optional must_contain substring). Categories: cognitive / fileio / notepad / calculator / explorer / browser / vscode / settings / multi-step / resilience / app (single-app coverage: Edge / Paint / Snipping Tool / Task Manager / VS Code / Clock / Downloads sort / Windows Terminal / Photos) / office (Word / Excel / PowerPoint / Outlook / OneNote — all dry-run, read-only or transient) / messaging (WeChat / Teams, dry-run only — never actually sends) / combo (multi-app integration) / self (Lucid operates its own UI: read sidebar, navigate /settings /memory /schedules /tools /templates pages, toggle theme — strictly read-only, never edits or deletes). Designed to run against a REAL desktop (whatever is open). Do NOT pre-clean. Messaging + office queries are dry-run: type/click but never save, never send; if app is not installed or not signed in, reply '<app> unavailable' and stop — that is a degraded pass.

queries.instruction: What is 137 * 24 + 9? Reply only with the number, no words.
Use read_file to open D:\Project\Lucid\README.md and tell me the very first H1 title (the line starting with a single '#'). Reply with just that title.

Use write_file to create %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-B1.txt with the single line 'hello from lucid e2e B1' (run_shell first if needed to create the Downloads\Test folder). Then reply 'task complete: wrote B1'.
Use read_file to open the file %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-B1.txt and reply with its exact content on a single line, then 'task complete:'.

Use run_shell to execute: powershell -Command "New-Item -ItemType File -Path $env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-B3.txt -Force | Out-Null; Remove-Item -LiteralPath $env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-B3.txt -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Test-Path $env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-B3.txt". Reply 'task complete: removed' if Test-Path printed False. (Note: this query intentionally targets its own B3 sentinel file, NOT B1's, so B1's deliverable survives for evaluation.)

Open Notepad, type the exact text 'Lucid E2E test C1', then save the file as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-C1.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed). After confirming the file exists via run_shell + Test-Path, reply 'task complete: notepad saved'.
Open Notepad and type today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Do NOT save. After typing, reply 'task complete: notepad typed'.

Open the Windows Calculator app and compute 137 * 24 + 9 inside it (click the buttons). Read the result shown in the display, then persist it: use run_shell to write the displayed number to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-D1.txt (e.g. `cmd /c echo <N> > %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-D1.txt`, or PowerShell `Set-Content`). Create the folder first via run_shell if needed. Reply 'task complete: calc=<number>'.

Open Calculator, switch to Scientific mode (View menu / hamburger). Make sure the angle unit is DEG (not RAD/GRAD), then compute sin(45) by clicking the buttons (`4`, `5`, then `sin`). Read the displayed result (should be approx 0.7071...). Persist it: use run_shell to write the displayed value to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-D2.txt (e.g. `cmd /c echo <value> > %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-D2.txt`, or PowerShell `Set-Content`; create the folder first via run_shell if needed). Reply 'task complete: sin45=<value>'.
Open File Explorer and navigate to D:\Project\Lucid. Tell me how many top-level subfolders are visible (folders only, not files). Reply 'task complete: <N> folders'.

Use run_shell (NOT GUI) to: (1) create folder %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-E2 if not exists, (2) confirm Test-Path is True, (3) remove it, (4) confirm Test-Path is False. Reply 'task complete: e2 cycle ok'.
Open Microsoft Edge to https://example.com . Wait for it to load, then describe in ONE sentence what the page contains. Reply 'task complete: <one sentence>'.

In Microsoft Edge, navigate to https://www.bing.com and search for 'Tauri 2 documentation'. Read the title of the first organic result (skip ads / 'People also ask' / 'Related searches') and reply 'task complete: first=<title>'.

Use the `read_webpage` meta-tool (headless, NOT the GUI) to fetch https://www.bing.com/search?q=Rust+programming+language and read the first organic result's title (skip ads / 'People also ask' / 'Related searches'). Reply 'task complete: first=<title>'.

Use run_shell to compute the line count of D:\Project\Lucid\lucid\sidecar.py via 'powershell -c (Get-Content -LiteralPath D:\Project\Lucid\lucid\sidecar.py).Length' and reply 'task complete: sidecar=<N> lines'.

Use run_shell to run 'powershell -c Select-String -Path D:\Project\Lucid\README.md -Pattern cron -SimpleMatch | Measure-Object | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Count' and reply 'task complete: matches=<N>'.
Open Windows Settings (Win+I), navigate to System > Display, and report the current display resolution shown there. Reply 'task complete: resolution=<WxH>'.
Open Settings (Win+I) and find 'About' (System > About). Report the Windows edition and version. Reply 'task complete: <edition> <version>'.

Step 1: Use run_shell to list all .py files directly inside D:\Project\Lucid\lucid (no subdirs). Step 2: Use write_file to save the resulting filename list (one per line) to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-I1.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed). Step 3: read_file it back and reply 'task complete: I1 wrote <N> entries'.
Take a fullscreen screenshot. From the taskbar at the bottom, list the pinned app icons left-to-right (skip the search box and Start button). Reply 'task complete: taskbar=<comma-separated names>'.

Try to launch an app called 'lucid_definitely_not_installed_app_xyz'. If launch_app reports it is not installed (or you cannot find it after one search), do NOT keep retrying. Reply 'task complete: app not installed' and stop.
Open Microsoft Edge and navigate to about:blank. Wait for the address bar to be empty, then read the window title via active_window. Reply 'task complete: edge title=<title>'.
Open Paint (mspaint). Once the canvas is visible, draw a single short stroke anywhere on the canvas using a left-button drag (any 2 points). Do NOT save. Reply 'task complete: paint stroke ok'.

Open the Snipping Tool (SnippingTool / ScreenSketch). When its toolbar is visible, take a screenshot of Lucid's own active_window so we can confirm it launched, then close it via Esc / close button. Reply 'task complete: snip launched'.

Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc). Switch to the Processes tab if not already there, and tell me the name of the top-most process row visible. Reply 'task complete: top proc=<name>'. Then close Task Manager.
Open Visual Studio Code. Once the welcome tab or last workspace is loaded, read the title bar via active_window and reply 'task complete: vscode=<title>'. Do not modify any file.
Open the Clock app (Windows clock / alarms) or the system tray clock flyout (click the time in the taskbar). Read the current time as displayed, reply 'task complete: clock=<HH:MM>'.

Use run_shell with 'powershell -c Get-ChildItem -Path $env:USERPROFILE\Downloads -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object Length -Descending | Select-Object -First 5 Name,Length | ConvertTo-Json -Compress' and reply 'task complete: top5 downloads=<json>'.

Open Windows Terminal (wt.exe). Once the prompt is visible, type the command 'Get-Date -Format "HH:mm"' and press Enter. Read the output time it printed and reply 'task complete: wt time=<HH:MM>'. If Windows Terminal is not installed, fall back to PowerShell (powershell.exe) with the same command, and reply 'task complete: wt time=<HH:MM>' anyway.

Open the Microsoft Photos app. Once its main window (collection / library) is visible, read the active_window title and reply 'task complete: photos title=<title>'. Do not open or modify any image. If Photos is not installed reply 'task complete: photos unavailable'.

Open Microsoft Word (winword). On the start screen click 'Blank document'. Once the empty document is visible, type the exact text 'Lucid E2E O1 dryrun'. Read the active_window title (should contain 'Word') and reply 'task complete: word title=<title>'. Do NOT save the document; if Word asks to save on close, click 'Don't Save'. If Word is not installed reply 'task complete: word unavailable'.

Open Microsoft Excel. Click 'Blank workbook'. Click cell A1, type the formula '=137*24+9' and press Enter. Click A1 again to re-select it, then read the displayed value (should be 3297). Reply 'task complete: excel a1=<value>'. Do NOT save. If Excel asks to save on close, click 'Don't Save'. If Excel is not installed reply 'task complete: excel unavailable'.

Open Microsoft PowerPoint. Click 'Blank Presentation'. On slide 1 click the 'Click to add title' placeholder, then type 'Lucid E2E O3'. Reply 'task complete: ppt slide ok'. Do NOT save. If PowerPoint asks to save on close, click 'Don't Save'. If PowerPoint is not installed reply 'task complete: ppt unavailable'.

Open Microsoft Outlook (the desktop app, not OWA). Once the Inbox is visible, report the subject of the topmost message in the message list. Do NOT open, reply, forward, mark-read, or delete any message. Reply 'task complete: outlook top=<subject>'. If Outlook is not installed or not signed in (no inbox visible after launch + 5s), reply 'task complete: outlook unavailable'.

Open Microsoft OneNote. Once a notebook page is visible (any existing page is fine), click in the page body and type the text 'Lucid E2E O5 dryrun'. OneNote auto-saves — that is fine. Reply 'task complete: onenote ok'. If OneNote is not installed or not signed in, reply 'task complete: onenote unavailable'.

Multi-app: (1) Open Notepad, (2) type 'Lucid combo L1 ' followed by today's YYYY-MM-DD date, (3) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-L1.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (4) close Notepad, (5) use read_file to verify the saved content begins with 'Lucid combo L1', (6) reply 'task complete: combo L1 ok'.

Multi-app: (1) Use Edge to open https://example.com, (2) once loaded, take an L2 screenshot of the active window, (3) describe the page in ONE short sentence, (4) use write_file to save that sentence to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-L2.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (5) reply 'task complete: combo L2 saved'.

Multi-app chain: (1) Use run_shell 'powershell -c (Get-Date).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")' to capture a timestamp, (2) Open Notepad, (3) type 'L3 timestamp <the captured value>', (4) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-L3.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (5) close Notepad, (6) reply 'task complete: combo L3 ok'.

Multi-app: (1) Take a fullscreen L1 screenshot, (2) count the number of apps pinned in the taskbar (excluding Start, Search, Widgets, Task View), (3) Open Notepad, (4) type 'taskbar pinned count = <N>', (5) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-L4.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (6) reply 'task complete: combo L4 N=<N>'.

Multi-app: (1) Open File Explorer to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test, (2) verify visually that the file 'lucid-e2e-L1.txt' is listed, (3) regardless of result, use run_shell + Test-Path to confirm, (4) reply 'task complete: combo L5 visual=<yes|no> shell=<True|False>'. Do not delete anything.

Open WeChat (微信). Once the main window is visible, find the conversation pinned at the very top of the chat list and report its display name. Do NOT open any conversation, do NOT send anything. Reply 'task complete: wechat top=<name>'. If WeChat is not installed or not logged in, reply 'task complete: wechat unavailable' and stop.

Open WeChat (微信), navigate to the 文件传输助手 (File Transfer) chat with yourself, type the exact text 'lucid-e2e-M2 ping' into the input box but do NOT press Send (do not click the send button, do not press Enter). Take a screenshot to confirm the text is in the box, then clear the input box (Ctrl+A, Delete). Reply 'task complete: wechat dryrun ok'. If WeChat is unavailable, reply 'task complete: wechat unavailable'.

Open Microsoft Teams. Once the main window is visible, report the name of the currently selected left-rail tab (Activity / Chat / Teams / Calendar / Files / etc.) and the count of unread badge numbers visible across the rail. Reply 'task complete: teams tab=<tab> unread=<N or none>'. If Teams is not installed or not logged in, reply 'task complete: teams unavailable'.

Open Microsoft Teams, click the Chat tab, find the chat with yourself (or the very first chat in the list if no self-chat exists), type 'lucid-e2e-M4 ping' into the message box, then press Enter to send (self-chat is safe and is the point of this test). Take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript. Reply 'task complete: teams sent ok'. If Teams is unavailable, reply 'task complete: teams unavailable'.

Multi-app messaging combo: (1) Use run_shell 'powershell -c (Get-Date).ToString("HH:mm")' to capture the current HH:MM, (2) Open WeChat and navigate to 文件传输助手, (3) type 'lucid-e2e-N1 ping at <HH:MM>' into the input box, (4) press Enter to send (文件传输助手 is your own clipboard chat — safe to send), (5) take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript, (6) reply 'task complete: combo N1 ok'. If WeChat is unavailable reply 'task complete: wechat unavailable'.

Multi-app: (1) Take a fullscreen screenshot, (2) describe in ONE short Chinese-or-English sentence what application currently has the foreground window, (3) Open Notepad, (4) type 'foreground at start = <that sentence>', (5) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-N2.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (6) reply 'task complete: combo N2 ok'. Do not change focus before step 1.

Multi-app cross-tool: (1) Open Microsoft Teams, (2) read the title of the active window via active_window, (3) Open Notepad, (4) type 'teams window title = <that title>', (5) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-N3.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (6) reply 'task complete: combo N3 ok'. If Teams is unavailable, instead type 'teams unavailable' into Notepad, save, and reply 'task complete: combo N3 fallback'.

Search-tool + Notepad combo (HEADLESS path): (1) Use `read_webpage` on https://www.bing.com/search?q=Python+official+site to fetch Bing results, (2) extract the FIRST organic result's title (skip ads / 'People also ask'), (3) open Notepad, (4) type 'P1 first = <that title>', (5) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-P1.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (6) reply 'task complete: combo P1 ok'.

Browser + Notepad combo (GUI path): (1) Open Microsoft Edge, (2) navigate to https://www.bing.com, (3) search for 'Rust programming language site:rust-lang.org' via the GUI search box, (4) read the FIRST organic result's title (any extraction method is fine), (5) open Notepad, (6) type 'P2 first = <that title>', (7) save as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-P2.txt (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (8) reply 'task complete: combo P2 ok'.

Search-tool + Excel combo (HEADLESS search + GUI Excel + save): (1) Use `read_webpage` on https://www.bing.com/search?q=Tauri+2+documentation to fetch Bing results headlessly, (2) extract the FIRST organic result's title AND its URL, (3) Open Microsoft Excel and click 'Blank workbook', (4) in A1 type the title, in B1 type the URL (press Tab between cells, Enter after B1), (5) save the workbook via Ctrl+S as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-P3.xlsx (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (6) reply 'task complete: combo P3 ok'. If Excel is not installed reply 'task complete: excel unavailable'.

Browser + Excel combo (GUI Bing + Excel + save): (1) Open Microsoft Edge, (2) navigate to https://www.bing.com and search 'Visual Studio Code release notes', (3) for the FIRST organic result read both the title and the URL, (4) Open Microsoft Excel → Blank workbook, (5) put the title in A1 and the URL in B1, (6) save via Ctrl+S as %USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Test\lucid-e2e-P4.xlsx (create the folder first via run_shell if needed), (7) reply 'task complete: combo P4 ok'. If Excel is unavailable reply 'task complete: excel unavailable'.

Search-tool + Teams combo (HEADLESS search + Teams self-send, end-to-end): (1) Use `read_webpage` on https://www.bing.com/search?q=Microsoft+Teams+keyboard+shortcuts to fetch Bing results, (2) extract the FIRST organic result's title, (3) Open Microsoft Teams, (4) click the Chat tab, find the chat with yourself (or the very first chat in the list if no self-chat exists), (5) type 'lucid-e2e-P5 first = <that title>' into the message box, (6) press Enter to send (self-chat is safe and is the point of this test), (7) take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript, (8) reply 'task complete: combo P5 ok'. If Teams is unavailable reply 'task complete: teams unavailable'.

Browser + Teams combo (GUI Bing + Teams self-send, end-to-end): (1) Open Microsoft Edge, (2) navigate to https://www.bing.com and search 'Microsoft Teams release notes', (3) for the FIRST organic result read its title, (4) Open Microsoft Teams, (5) click the Chat tab, find the chat with yourself (or the first chat in the list if no self-chat exists), (6) type 'lucid-e2e-P6 first = <that title>' into the message box, (7) press Enter to send (self-chat is safe), (8) take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript, (9) reply 'task complete: combo P6 ok'. If Teams is unavailable reply 'task complete: teams unavailable'.

Search-tool + WeChat combo (HEADLESS search + WeChat self-send, end-to-end): (1) Use `read_webpage` on https://www.bing.com/search?q=Rust+programming+language to fetch Bing results, (2) extract the FIRST organic result's title, (3) Open WeChat (微信), (4) navigate to the 文件传输助手 (File Transfer) chat with yourself, (5) type 'lucid-e2e-P7 first = <that title>' into the input box, (6) press Enter to send (文件传输助手 is your own clipboard chat — safe to send), (7) take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript, (8) reply 'task complete: combo P7 ok'. If WeChat is not installed or not logged in, reply 'task complete: wechat unavailable'.

Browser + WeChat combo (GUI Bing + WeChat self-send, end-to-end): (1) Open Microsoft Edge, (2) navigate to https://www.bing.com and search 'Python official site', (3) for the FIRST organic result read its title, (4) Open WeChat (微信), (5) navigate to 文件传输助手, (6) type 'lucid-e2e-P8 first = <that title>' into the input box, (7) press Enter to send (self-chat is safe), (8) take a screenshot to confirm the sent message appears in the chat transcript, (9) reply 'task complete: combo P8 ok'. If WeChat is unavailable reply 'task complete: wechat unavailable'.

Focus the Lucid app window itself (window title contains 'Lucid'). Use focus_window if needed. Take an active_window screenshot. In the left sidebar there is a 'Conversations' list — count the number of conversation entries currently visible (do NOT scroll, just what's on screen). Reply 'task complete: lucid conversations=<N>'. Do NOT click + new thread, do NOT delete any conversation, do NOT type into the input box.

Focus the Lucid window. In the top header click the gear / Settings link to open the /settings page. Once the page is visible, read the currently selected 'Provider' value (anthropic / copilot / proxy) and report it. Then navigate back to the chat by clicking '← Back to chat' (or the equivalent home / chat link). Reply 'task complete: lucid provider=<value>'. Do NOT change any setting, do NOT click Save.

Focus the Lucid window. Open the /memory page (navigate via the header / sidebar nav link labelled 'Memory' or 'memory.md'). Once visible, count the number of long-term memory bullet/list entries currently shown and report it. Then go back to chat. Reply 'task complete: lucid memory=<N> entries'. Do NOT add, edit, or delete any entry.

Focus the Lucid window. Open the /schedules page via the nav link labelled 'Schedules'. Report how many scheduled tasks are listed and how many of them are currently enabled (the ones with the toggle on). Then go back to chat. Reply 'task complete: lucid schedules total=<T> enabled=<E>'. Do NOT add, edit, delete, run, pause, or resume any schedule.

Focus the Lucid window. In the top header find the theme toggle button (🌙 or ☀️). Read the current theme by checking which icon is showing (☀️ means current theme is dark, 🌙 means current theme is light — the icon shows what you'd switch TO). Click the button ONCE to switch theme; observe the page colours change; then click it AGAIN to switch back to the original theme. Reply 'task complete: lucid theme cycled, original=<light|dark>'.

Focus the Lucid window. Open the /tools page (or 'Tips' / 'tools.md' nav link). Count how many operation tip entries are listed (one per line / bullet) and report it. Then go back to chat. Reply 'task complete: lucid tools=<N> tips'. Do NOT edit or delete any tip.

Focus the Lucid window. Open the /templates page via its nav link. Report the names of all templates currently saved (comma-separated). If none, reply '<empty>'. Then go back to chat. Reply 'task complete: lucid templates=<list-or-empty>'. Do NOT run, edit, delete, or create any template.
